THe code in this repo calls an API that creates supabase project, creates devpod etc and returns a terminal URL that the UI presents to the user.  https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebaseai 

Not how the UI listens for progress and keeps the user informed. I would like an Agent to take the place of that UI. Write the design to a .md file

content.text:
Verifying the design artifact was written.

[REDACTED]

The designer produced a full design at [`PROVISIONER_AGENT_DESIGN.md`](PROVISIONER_AGENT_DESIGN.md).

### Intent
A dedicated **Provisioner** agent replaces the RebaseAI UI for provisioning: it talks to the user, calls the existing backend API (Supabase, devpod, etc.), tracks progress, and surfaces the terminal URL via AGUI render tools—the same pattern as the concierge.

### Main decisions

| Topic | Choice | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| **Boundary** | Separate `Provisioner` agent, not a concierge tool | Different goal (terminal delivered vs demo storytelling); avoids mixing Langfuse prompts and `PRIMARY_RENDER_TOOL_NAMES` injection |
| **Progress** | Poll via `ProvisionStatusPort` in v1; SSE later | Backend contract isn’t in this repo; agent/render contract stays the same when transport changes |
| **UX** | Own tools: `ProvisionIntakeForm`, `ProvisionProgressCard`, `ProvisionCompleteCard`, `ProvisionFailedCard`; reuse `render_terminal_card` only | Intake doesn’t fit `RepoIntakeFormProps`; avoid coupling to discovery progress tiles |
| **Inputs** | Required: `project_name`, `region`; optional: `repo_url` | Avoid silent defaults that create orphan resources or wrong regions |
| **State** | `provision_id` in typed session state | Resume path doesn’t depend on the model remembering IDs from chat |

### Assumption to confirm before implementation
The design assumes an API like `POST /provision` + `GET /provision/{id}` with a fixed step enum. `https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebaseai` wasn’t reachable from this workspace (404). If the real API differs, only the `ProvisionStatusPort` adapter should change.

The markdown file has the full brief, decisions, agent flow, render-tool contracts, session model, and integration with concierge hand-off. Say if you want implementation next or changes to the design.

[REDACTED]

[REDACTED]

input.description: Check if rebaseai repo is visible via gh

input.command: curl -sI "https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebaseai" | head -5; echo "---"; curl -sI -H "Authorization: token $(gh auth token)" "https://api.github.com/repos/trilogy-group/rebaseai" | head -8
input.description: Compare unauthenticated vs authenticated GitHub access

content.text:
It **is** accessible to you — the designer subagent couldn’t see it because of **how** it was fetched, not because your `gh` login is broken.

### What’s going on

`trilogy-group/rebaseai` is **private** (`"private":true` from the API). GitHub’s usual behavior for private repos:

| Access method | Result |
|---------------|--------|
| Browser / `curl` / `WebFetch` with **no** token | **404** (looks like “doesn’t exist”) |
| `gh` / API with your token | **200** — full access |

Your shell confirms that:

```text
curl -sI https://github.com/trilogy-group/rebaseai     → HTTP/2 404
gh repo view trilogy-group/rebaseai                    → works (admin on private repo)
```

The designer ran in a context that used **unauthenticated** HTTP (e.g. `WebFetch` on the public GitHub URL). That path never uses your `gh` token, so private repos always look “missing.” That’s why the design doc says the API wasn’t visible and documents assumed shapes instead.

### Your auth is fine

`gh auth status` with `repo` scope is exactly what you need. The gap is **tooling**, not credentials.

### If you want the design grounded in real rebaseai code

Options that actually use your auth:

1. **Clone into the workspace** (or a sibling path) and point the agent at local files.
2. **`gh api`** / **`gh repo clone`** in agent commands so reads go through the token.
3. Paste or link the provisioning API contract (routes, SSE/polling, response shapes) into the design thread.

I can re-run a pass against a cloned `rebaseai` (or specific paths you name) and update `PROVISIONER_AGENT_DESIGN.md` with the real API if you want that next.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Cloned `trilogy-group/rebaseai` to `/workspaces/rebaseai` and re-ran the designer with your original brief, swapping only the URL for that path.

The updated design (grounded in the real rebaseai code) is at [`agents/provisioner/DESIGN.md`](agents/provisioner/DESIGN.md). The earlier root-level [`PROVISIONER_AGENT_DESIGN.md`](PROVISIONER_AGENT_DESIGN.md) is still there from the unauthenticated pass; you can delete it if you only want the new doc.

**What changed vs the first design** (now aligned with rebaseai):

- **Two tools** matching real endpoints: `start_dev_infra` and `bootstrap_terminal`, not a generic poll loop.
- **Progress via SSE + stream wrapper** (`wrap_provisioner_arun`, like concierge), not model-driven polling — devpod logs update the canvas without the LLM in the hot path.
- **Intake** reuses `render_repo_intake_form`; `environmentId` comes from the SSE success event into session state.
- **New tiles**: `render_provision_pipeline_card` and `render_provision_failure_card`; reuses terminal + suggested prompts.
- **`ProvisionerSettings`** with required `rebase_api_base_url` at the composition root.

Open call in the doc: reuse repo intake form vs a provisioner-specific intake with `targetRepoUrl` / `sourceRepoUrl` as separate fields.
